title: Build an Autonomous AI Recruiting Investigator
description: An autonomous multi-agent system that investigates candidates by discovering and analyzing their public technical work (GitHub, hackathons, Kaggle, portfolios), extracts genuine-passion signals, compares each candidate's projects to a target company project using embeddings and vector search, ranks candidates, and produces an evidence-backed executive summary. The system must reason like an investigator: collect evidence, cite sources, and never fabricate findings.
business_problem: Hiring teams cannot reliably tell who is genuinely passionate about a problem versus who merely tailored a resume. Resume screeners miss builders who have been quietly working on the exact problem for years.
target_users: Technical recruiters, hiring managers, and founders at AI-first companies hiring agent/ML engineers.
expected_features: Autonomous evidence discovery across multiple public sources
Embedding-based project-to-project similarity with vector search
Passion / genuine-interest scoring from public artifacts
Candidate ranking with transparent, evidence-cited explanations
Executive summary report or video generation
success_criteria: Recommends candidates whose prior public projects clearly match the target problem, with every recommendation backed by verifiable source URLs and zero fabricated evidence.
